work items may be immediately deducted from the amount due to the contractors
pursuant to Section 5, Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (c)
regularly monitor and inspect infrastructure assets of the municipal government
particularly those that are still within the defects liability period and the applicable
warranty period for structural defects so that the Municipal Mayor can
immediately demand repair or reconstruction works on damaged portions thereof
from the contractors or any party found to be liable therefor pursuant to Section
62.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer immediately submit the documents required for
technical evaluation and inspection of infrastructure projects implemented “by
administration” totaling P22,805,944.01 and projects implemented “by
contract” totaling P33,887,369.27 including explanation on the variances
between the contract amounts and items of work and the lacking technical
documents of the Construction of Evacuation Center Phase III project;
b. Municipal Accountant review the subsidiary ledgers and record necessary
adjustments to Construction-in-Progress, Infrastructure Assets and Buildings
and Other Structures accounts to determine the total cost incurred for each
project and henceforth see to it that disbursements on infrastructure projects are
supported with sufficient technical documents and that vouchers and supporting
documents of completed projects are immediately submitted for audit; and
c. Municipal Mayor ensure that the required reports and documents are
submitted by the concerned municipal government officials to the Audit Team
in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission pursuant to the
provisions of Sections 39 and 122 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer immediately submit the required Revised
PERT/CPM/S-Curve of the Concreting of Municipal Pathway project, compute for
liquidated damages and submit report on the cause of delay. He should likewise
submit an explanation on why award of contract to a pakyaw group organized by a
contractor was recommended for Construction of Multi-Purpose Hall in Bgy.
Barotuan National High School and henceforth ensure strict compliance with the
provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 dated December 6, 2006 on pakyaw
contracting procedures;
b. BAC submit an explanation on why award of contract to Palawan Narra
Commercial was recommended even though the bid price was higher than the
ABC and to E. M. Abin Trading and Construction even though the bidding
documents were not signed by the bidder himself or by his duly authorized
representative. The BAC should henceforth ensure strict compliance with the
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR; and
c. Municipal government undertake the necessary repair works on the damaged
portions of the projects and demand full reimbursement of the expenses from the
project engineers if defects were determined to be caused by poor workmanship
under their supervision.

We recommended that the 1) Municipal Accountant record the necessary
adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-
DRRMF account and henceforth ensure that relief goods, whether donated or
purchased, are recorded and reported using the perpetual inventory method as
prescribed by the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and 2) Municipal Accountant,
MDRRMO and Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate transfer of the
unexpended amount of P24,469,935.12 to a separate depository account for
DRRMF.

We recommended that the a) Market Manager ensure that lease of market
stalls are covered by lease contracts stipulating the stall reference numbers, area of
stall being leased, amount and duration of lease and strictly enforce the terms and
provisions of the lease contracts and of the Revenue Code; b) Municipal Treasurer
immediately demand payment of the cash bond, rental fees and surcharges totaling
P191,445.00 as of December 31, 2018, submit proof of collection and deposit thereof
to the Audit Team, reconstruct the record of collections of the 33 lessees and
henceforth, collect monthly rental fees on or before the due date stipulated in the
Revenue Code; and c) License Inspector conduct annual inspection of business
establishments to ensure collection of Mayor’s permit fees in compliance with the
provisions of Sections 3A.01 and 3A.02 of the Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) immediately secure, through
authorized representative, the required water permits from the NWRB and submit
authenticated copies thereof to the Audit Team; b) request for NWRB inspection of
the completed water supply systems and obtain Certificates of Compliance therefor;
c) submit the water tariffs to the NWRB for approval; and d) ensure that land titles
or other tenurial instruments for the project site and necessary permits and licenses
are secured prior to implementation of projects.
Further, we recommended that the MEEDO institute actions to collect the
receivables from water bills totaling P1,094,569.30 at year end, ensure that the
provisions of Section 10 of the ENWSS Management Ordinance are complied with
and propose amendment to ENWSS Management Ordinance to stipulate the due
date of water bills in terms of number of days from delivery of meter reading.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
prioritize the preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan for approval
of the Palawan Council for Sustainable Development. We also recommended that
additional funds from the Eco-Tourism Development Fee (ETDF) collections be
appropriated for improvement of the municipal government’s solid waste
management system and sanitary landfill, establishment of MRF in every barangay
or clusters of barangay and public education and information activities on
ecological waste management.

We recommended that the a) GFPS submit for audit the Annual GPB and
GAD AR within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March
18, 2014; and b) Local Finance Committee and the GFPS ensure that the Annual
GPB is prepared in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA
JMC No. No. 2013-01, as amended, and incorporated in the Annual Budget as
reviewed and endorsed by the DILG to ensure gender responsiveness of the GAD
programs and projects.

We recommended that the a) Municipal Mayor require the immediate
settlement of disallowances and enforcement of COEs; b) Municipal Accountant
reconcile the subsidiary ledgers (SLs) against the Official Receipts (ORs) issued for
settlement of disallowances, take up the necessary adjustments to the account
Receivables-Disallowances/Charges and transmit the updated/adjusted SLs and
proof of settlement of NDs to the Audit Team for issuance of NSSDC; and c)
Municipal Treasurer indicate the reference numbers of NDs in the ORs issued for
settlement of disallowances for proper recording in the books of accounts.
We recommended for the BAC to see to it that the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR on advertisement and posting of procurement-related notices are complied with.
observations.reason: There were still  awarded contracts  which were not  posted in the  PhilGEPs website  in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Section 347 of RA No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the Office of the Municipal Accountant submitted additional vouchers and supporting documents for recorded transactions in calendar years 2016 to 2017.
observations.reason: Reiterated in     Finding No. 12 of this AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant review the Memoranda of Agreement pertaining to the aforesaid unexpended balances and communicate with the source agencies for the immediate return of such balances.

We recommended that a) funds be appropriated for the survey and titling of
land; b) the Municipal General Services Officer be instructed to inventory all real
properties of the municipal government whether acquired, donated or reclaimed
and to institute proceedings for issuance of tax declarations and certificates of title
in the name of the municipal government in coordination with the Municipal
Assessor; ¢) the Municipal General Services Officer be instructed to regularly
inspect the real properties owned by the municipal government to keep track of
their condition; and d) full payment for acquisition of land should only be made to
the property owner at the time of transfer of title to the municipal government if
whole lot is acquired or at the time of the annotation of a deed of sale on the title if
only portion of the lot is acquired.

We recommended that a) the Inventory Committee be required to complete
the actual physical inventory, reconcile the results of the count with available
records in the accounting and general services office and submit the RPCI and
RPCPPE to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I, and that all discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be
investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately; and b) the accounting and general
services office be required to reconstruct the property cards and ledgers based on
source documents or vouchers to facilitate reconciliation of physical and book
inventories.

We recommended for the accounting office to reconstruct the
maintenance/repair record of each equipment/vehicle owned and/or operated by the
municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to obtain copies of subsidiary ledgers on cash accounts and bank
statements from the Municipal Accountant, immediately reconcile the same with the
cashbook balances and record the necessary adjusting entries in the cashbooks as of
December 31, 2017 and submit a reconciliation statement to the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately locate and submit to the Municipal Accountant the
disbursement vouchers, obligations requests and supporting documents to the
checks totaling £140,667.11 for the disbursements to be properly recorded in the
books.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the required
reports on projects/programs/activities are submitted within the period prescribed
in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 as amended by COA
Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015. The monitoring report on the
municipal government’s projects/programs/activities should contain details as to
project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual
completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date duly reconciled with
the accounting records.

We recommended that a) the Municipal Treasurer be required to update the Real Property
Tax Account Registers or Taxpayer’s Index Card, prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accountant the certified list of taxpayers showing the total amount due and collectible in
the ensuing years; and b) the Municipal Accountant be required to set up the Real
Property Tax Receivable, Special Education Tax Receivable, Deferred Real Property Tax
Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income accounts for CY 2018 and onwards
based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer showing the total amount
of tax due and collectible.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to locate and
submit the following documents relevant to the unliquidated cash advances for filing
of appropriate cases against erring accountable officers:

a. Authenticated copy of Appointment of the Accountable Officer (AO)
granted cash advance

b. Designation or authority to draw cash advance

c. Authenticated copy of AO’s fidelity bond

d. Authenticated copy of demand letter of the local chief executive and proof of
receipt by the AO and answer/explanation thereto

e. Disbursement voucher/s and supporting documents pertinent to cash
advances granted to the AO/employee/recipient or equivalent and partial
liquidation documents

f. Paid check for the cash advance

g. Other relevant documents

In all instances, the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant must
ensure that each provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 are strictly observed and
that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to reconcile
the subject reciprocal accounts, determine the cause of disparity in balances and
record the necessary adjustments in the books to present the correct balances in the
financial statements.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the Annual
Investment Plan and Annual Budget are duly supported with a List of PPAs for the
LCPC fully compliant with the provisions of Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG
MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 and prepared in accordance with the format
prescribed in Annex E-4 of the 2016 Budget Operations Manual for LGUs.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the accounting and
budget offices to reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly and
prepare and submit the required reconciliation report at year-end.

We recommended that:

a. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the Local
Development Council include in the Annual Investment Plan only those
programs and projects which are feasible, specific and properly identified as to
target date of execution and the personnel in-charge or responsible for the
implementation;

b. the MPDC coordinate with the Municipal Engineer so that infrastructure
projects are supported with Program of Works (POW) and Detailed Estimates
to ensure optimum utilization of the funds and timely and -efficient
implementation of the projects;

¢. the end-user units appropriately schedule the procurement in the preparation of
Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) and Annual Procurement Plan
(APP) in a way that would allow project implementation and completion within
the year that it was programmed and provided with appropriations;

d. the Municipal Mayor require the Members of the BAC and TWG to give utmost
priority to BAC assignments so that procurements are completed on schedule;

e. the Municipal Mayor, through the MPDC, monitor and evaluate implementation
of the different development programs, projects and activities of the municipal
government in accordance with the approved development plan; and

f. the Municipal Mayor, through the MPDC, to see to it that the 20% Development
Fund is utilized for allowable development projects in accordance with the
provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended for the Local Finance Committee to conduct the necessary
review of continuing appropriations as part of the annual budget preparation to
determine unexpended balances that may be re-appropriated to fund other priority
programs and projects of the municipal government.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the lacking technical
documents. Henceforth, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended for municipal government officials to adopt austerity
measures and exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds and to
strictly observe the provisions of Section 325(h), 343 and 447 of RA No. 7160 to
avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that the end-user or implementing units be required to
prepare and submit for consolidation the PPMPs for the different programs,
activities and projects including those provided with special purpose appropriations
and that, prior to the approval of the budget, an APP be accurately prepared
containing all the information required in the format prescribed by GPPB to
promote meticulous and judicious procurement planning and appropriate
timing/phasing of activities.

We recommended for the BAC to see to it that the provisions of RA No. 9184
and its IRR on advertisement and posting of procurement-related notices are
complied with.

We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Section 347 of
Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.

We recommended that 1) the Municipal Mayor require the immediate
settlement of disallowances and enforcement of COEs; 2) the Municipal Accountant
reconcile the subsidiary ledgers (SLs) against the Official Receipts (ORs) issued for
settlement of disallowances, take up the necessary adjustments to the account
Receivables-Disallowances/Charges and transmit the updated/adjusted SLs and
proof of settlement of NDs to the Auditor for issuance of NSSDC; and 3) the
Municipal Treasurer indicate the reference numbers of NDs in the ORs issued for
settlement of disallowances for proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant
review the Memoranda of Agreement pertaining to the aforesaid unexpended
balances and communicate with the source agencies for the immediate return of
such balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 1) secure copies of Memoranda
of Agreement entered into and signed in behalf of the municipal government and all
its annexes and supporting documents; and 2) furnish the department head or
official in-charge of implementing the projects and the Municipal Accountant copies
of such documents prior to implementation to ensure that the trust funds are
utilized in accordance with the trust agreements.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the lacking documents.
Henceforth, we recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Section 6.5 of
COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.3 of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that 1) the Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile
and update the records on Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; and 2) the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that funds collected by the
municipal government in behalf of other government agencies are remitted to them
fully and within the due dates set to avoid incurrence of interests and penalties.

We recommended for the Local Finance Committee to see to it that at least
5% of the municipal government’s total budget are appropriated for GAD and for
the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to monitor and ensure the effective and
efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD
budget.

We recommended for the Local Finance Committee and the GFPS to ensure
that the GAD Plan and Budget is prepared in accordance with the provisions of the
aforesaid Joint Memorandum Circulars and incorporated in the Annual Budget as
reviewed by the DILG to ensure gender responsiveness of the GAD programs and
projects.
